_note: MOCK procedure result for PT-022. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Clinically consistent with pre-procedure referral (positive FIT + new hematochezia + mild anemia; DAPT after recent DES).

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Positive FIT with new intermittent hematochezia and mild anemia (Hgb 12.3 g/dL); evaluate colonic bleeding source
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (propofol), anesthesia-directed given cardiac history
procedure.prep_quality: Boston Bowel Prep Scale 8/9 (right 2, transverse 3, left 3) — adequate
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve); terminal ileum intubated
procedure.periprocedural_antithrombotic: Coordinated with cardiology prior to procedure given drug-eluting stents placed Dec 2025 (<12 months). Aspirin 81 mg CONTINUED throughout. Ticagrelor 90 mg BID held for 3 days pre-procedure per cardiology instruction; cardiology approved short hold with plan to resume promptly. No bridging. INR 1.0; not on systemic anticoagulant.

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with polypectomy, endoscopic mucosal resection, and prophylactic clip placement. INDICATION: Positive FIT plus new intermittent hematochezia and mild anemia in a 62-year-old man; high pretest concern for a colonic source. ANTITHROMBOTIC: High-risk cardiac patient with two drug-eluting stents (Dec 2025) on dual antiplatelet therapy. Periprocedural plan coordinated with cardiology: aspirin continued; ticagrelor held 3 days pre-procedure with cardiology approval and a plan for prompt resumption. SEDATION: MAC/propofol, anesthesia-directed. PREP: adequate (BBPS 8). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum and the terminal ileum was intubated and normal. In the sigmoid colon a 22 mm laterally spreading, granular sessile lesion was identified; it was lifted with submucosal injection and removed piecemeal by endoscopic mucosal resection. The resection base appeared complete; two prophylactic hemostatic clips were placed given continued aspirin and pending ticagrelor resumption. A separate 6 mm sessile polyp in the descending colon was removed by cold snare. Moderate sigmoid diverticulosis was present without active bleeding or stigmata. Small non-bleeding internal hemorrhoids were noted on retroflexion in the rectum. No active bleeding at completion; adequate hemostasis confirmed. IMPRESSION: (1) 22 mm sigmoid laterally spreading lesion removed by piecemeal EMR, clips placed — pathology pending, concerning for advanced adenoma; (2) 6 mm descending colon polyp removed; (3) diverticulosis; (4) small internal hemorrhoids (likely source of bright red blood on tissue). The sigmoid lesion is the probable source of the positive FIT and anemia. Pathology pending. Delayed post-polypectomy bleeding risk elevated on continued aspirin and resumed antiplatelet therapy.

findings.finding: 22 mm laterally spreading granular sessile lesion
findings.action_taken: piecemeal endoscopic mucosal resection with submucosal lift; 2 prophylactic hemostatic clips placed
none (likely source of bright red blood per rectum)

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort after uneventful recovery. Post-polypectomy and post-EMR precautions reviewed. Given DES <12 months, cardiology-directed antiplatelet resumption emphasized. Return precautions for significant rectal bleeding, lightheadedness, or chest pain.

followup.recommendation: Review sigmoid EMR and descending colon polyp pathology; call patient with results and set surveillance interval
Coordinate with cardiology to resume ticagrelor 90 mg BID promptly after procedure given drug-eluting stents <12 months; balance stent-thrombosis risk against post-EMR delayed bleeding risk
Monitor for delayed post-EMR/post-polypectomy bleeding on continued aspirin plus resumed ticagrelor; patient counseled on warning signs
Repeat CBC to reassess mild anemia after treating colonic source; consider iron studies
Plan surveillance colonoscopy at ~6 months to confirm complete resection of piecemeal EMR site

planted_followup_items.description: 22 mm sigmoid laterally spreading lesion removed by piecemeal EMR — pathology pending; concerning for advanced adenoma, possible high-grade dysplasia or worse. Result drives surveillance interval and any need for further resection/referral.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review pathology within 1 week, call patient with result, document surveillance/repeat-EMR plan

planted_followup_items.description: Ticagrelor 90 mg BID was held 3 days pre-procedure. Patient has two drug-eluting stents placed <12 months ago (Dec 2025) — high stent-thrombosis risk with any prolonged antiplatelet interruption. Resumption must be coordinated with cardiology and not left dropped.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm with cardiology and instruct patient to resume ticagrelor promptly (24-48h) unless active bleeding; document resumption date

planted_followup_items.description: Elevated delayed post-EMR/post-polypectomy bleeding risk on continued aspirin plus resumed ticagrelor after a 22 mm piecemeal sigmoid EMR with clips.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: counsel on warning signs; ensure patient can reach GI; low threshold for evaluation of rebleeding for up to 2 weeks

planted_followup_items.description: Piecemeal EMR of the sigmoid lesion requires a surveillance colonoscopy at ~6 months to confirm complete resection of the EMR scar.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule 6-month surveillance colonoscopy once pathology confirms adenoma; coordinate periprocedural antiplatelet plan with cardiology

planted_followup_items.description: Overall colonoscopy surveillance interval pending final pathology of sigmoid EMR and descending polyp specimens.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: set and document interval once pathology finalizes

planted_followup_items.description: Mild anemia (Hgb 12.3 g/dL) with an identified colonic bleeding source now treated; requires confirmation that hemoglobin recovers after resection.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: repeat CBC in 4-6 weeks; consider iron studies; escalate if anemia worsens

planted_followup_items.description: Small non-bleeding internal hemorrhoids and sigmoid diverticulosis noted; hemorrhoids are the likely source of the bright red blood on tissue.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: conservative management counseling; no urgent action

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat's a little scratchy and I'm burping and bloated from all the air. Otherwise okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation bloating; no red flags
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected and self-limited over 24-48h; walking and warm fluids help.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-colonoscopy symptoms; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The heart office said I could start my Brilinta back up. Is that alright after they took that big polyp, and when do I hear about the biopsy?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient confirming ticagrelor resumption timing (held 3 days pre-procedure, DES <12 months) and asking about pending EMR pathology
contacts.expected_action: Confirm cardiology-directed prompt resumption of ticagrelor is appropriate given DES <12 months; counsel that this raises delayed post-EMR bleeding risk so watch for warning signs; note pathology expected ~day 6 and the team will call with results and surveillance interval.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the antithrombotic plan and follow-up items; links to med_resume (F2) and touches pending pathology (F1).

contacts.verbatim: I've passed a good amount of dark red blood and some clots into the toilet twice this afternoon and I'm feeling dizzy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-EMR/post-polypectomy hemorrhage after 22 mm piecemeal sigmoid EMR, on continued aspirin plus resumed ticagrelor, with orthostatic/hemodynamic symptom
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate ED evaluation; assess hemodynamics/CBC; hold ticagrelor pending evaluation and coordinate urgently with cardiology given DES; anticipate repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis at the clipped EMR site.
contacts.rationale: Delayed post-EMR hemorrhage with lightheadedness is a genuine red-flag complication amplified by resumed antiplatelet therapy — the anticipated one_complication event for this high-risk EMR.

contacts.verbatim: I saw a little pink on the toilet paper after a bowel movement today. No dizziness, feeling fine. Should I worry?
contacts.clinical_signal: Minor streaking on tissue consistent with known internal hemorrhoids; no volume of blood, no hemodynamic symptoms, well outside the highest-risk delayed-bleed window
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scant pink on tissue is consistent with the documented internal hemorrhoids; advise fiber/hydration and to call back or seek care if there is a larger volume of dark red blood, clots, or lightheadedness.
contacts.rationale: Dangerous-sounding-but-benign triage trap — a small amount of pink on the tissue in an asymptomatic patient with known hemorrhoids should be reassured, not escalated, and contrasts with the true bleed in C3.
